source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
The perimeter of the rectangle $A B C D$ is $30 \mathrm{~cm}$. Three more rectangles are added so that their centres are in the corners A, B and D and their sides are parallel to the rectangle (see diagram). The sum of the perimeters of these three rectangles is $20 \mathrm{~cm}$. What is the length of the boarder of the shape (thick black line)?
<image1>

image_dependency: The target is the length of the thick black border of a composite shape, which depends on the geometric arrangement shown in the image
Description involves rectangle corners A, B, D and added rectangles with parallel sides, suggesting spatial/diagram interpretation is central
multi_step: Requires combining the given perimeter of rectangle ABCD (30 cm) with the summed perimeters of three added rectangles (20 cm)
Solving likely needs reasoning about which edges contribute to the outer border versus internal/shared edges
This is not a direct lookup; it involves geometric composition and perimeter accounting across multiple shapes
verifiability: Answer is a precise numeric length ('$40 cm$'), which is easy to check against a correct derivation
Multiple-choice options are provided, enabling straightforward outcome verification
asset_registry_record indicates question and answer are present and registry_passed=true

decision_reasons: Question, answer, and required diagram are all present, and the asset registry passed with no recorded issues.
The sample is strongly image-dependent, but the image asset exists, so the core semantic content is intact and recoverable.
Initial scoring is strong overall, with high verifiability and solid multi-step reasoning value.
